LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FRANCES SUSSNA'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS TO PALO ALTO MEDICAL FOUNDATION, GREGORY ENGLE, M.D., DR. CONRAD MASSIMO VIAL, AND DR. GRAHAM BEAUMONT AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED, IN PART. THE SUBPOENA TO PALO ALTO MEDICAL FOUNDATION (PAMF) SHALL BE QUASHED, BUT MAY BE MODIFIED AND REISSUED PURSUANT TO A COURT ORDER. THE SUBPOENA TO PAMF, PLAINTIFF'S GENERAL PURPOSE HEALTHCARE PROVIDER, IS OVERBROAD IN SEEKING "ANY AND ALL" RECORDS WITHOUT RESTRICTION AS TO TYPE OF MEDICAL CONDITION, SCOPE OR TIME. THE SUBPOENA TO PAMF SHALL BE LIMITED TO CARDIAC (HEART) TREATMENT (INCLUDING STRESS TESTS, HEART DIAGNOSIS, TREATMENT AND PROCEDURES), OBESITY, SHORTNESS OF BREATH, BLOOD PRESSURE AND NEUROLOGICAL ISSUES (INCLUDING MYOCLONUS). THE MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS DIRECTED SPECIFICALLY TO DR. VIAL AND DR. ENGLE, PLAINTIFF'S CARDIAC SURGEON AND CARDIOLOGIST, RESPECTIVELY, IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLACED HER HEART CONDITION AND HEART SURGERY AT ISSUE. THE PARTIES ARE TO MEET AND CONFER AND ATTEMPT TO AGREE REGARDING THE TIME LIMITATION, IF ANY, TO BE PLACED ON THESE SUBPOENAS. AS TO THE SUBPOENA TO DR. GRAHAM BEAUMONT, IF PLAINTIFF CAN CLARIFY AND STIPULATE THAT: (1) SHE DOES NOT CLAIM SEVERE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS OVER AND ABOVE THAT WHICH WOULD ORDINARILY BE ASSOCIATED WITH AN INJURY OF THIS TYPE; AND (2) AT TRIAL SHE WILL CLAIM NO ECONOMIC DAMAGES FOR THERAPY BILLS AND WILL NOT PRESENT EXPERT TESTIMONY BY ANY MENTAL HEALTH PROVIDER AT TRIAL, THEN THE COURT WILL QUASH THE SUBPOENA TO DR. GRAHAM BEAUMONT (PSYCHIATRIST). IF SHE DOES NOT SO CLARIFY, A HEARING WILL BE REQUIRED ON THE SUBPOENA TO DR. BEAUMONT. MONETARY SANCTIONS ARE DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DAVID I. SIEGEL, ESQ. of Grellas Shah LLP, Tel No. 408-255-6310 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. TRACY A. TUCKER, ESQ. of Homan & Stone, Tel No. 909-307-9380 appearing for Defendants Prime Old County L.P. and Prime Old County Acquisition LLC.

RULING - Matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to Plaintiff Frances Sussna's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents By Stephen Sturla And Daniel Marc Bornstein And Exclusion Of Expert Testimony, the motion is granted as to document requests to Bornstein 18, 19 (if available) and 20. As to request 6: Bornstein must provide a list of all publications and, if not otherwise reasonably available to plaintiff from other sources, provide copies on request at plaintiff's expense. As to 21: Bornstein must provide documents sufficient to show what percentage of his annual income is derived from Bay Property Group. The motion granted as to document requests to Sturla: 3, 8, 18, 19, 20. As to 6, Sturla must provide a list of all publications and, if not otherwise reasonably available to plaintiff from other sources, provide copies on request at plaintiff's expense. Defendants failed to provide admissible evidence that production of the requested documents would be burdensome.
Sanctions awarded against defendants in the amount of $3,000. Sanctions payable within 30 days of the Court's order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Jennifer M. Rodrigues, CSR 484, Tel No. 510-888-9825 (302/EHG)

The Court announces his tentative rulings and orders the 17 questions to be answered in 7 hours. ARGUED. Defedant contests sanctions. Both parties submit to the tentative rulings. Parties are ordered back in the hallway to discuss the orders. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS PRIME OLD COUNTY LP and PRIME OLD COUNTY ACQUISITIONS, LLC's Motion For Protective Order is GRANTED to govern the conduct of the person Most Knowledgeable Deposition as follows: (1) Defendants must properly educate their designated PMQ before the continuation of the deposition (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390);
(2) Both counsel are directed to keep colloquy to a bare minimum and avoid any personal attacks. In particular, defense counsel shall not instruct the witness not to answer any questions except on privilege. Any objections shall be limited to the legal basis for the objection (i.e. "calls for speculation") without any other commentary; (3) Plaintiff's counsel shall streamline his examination to prevent any duplicative and repetitive questioning; (4) Plaintiff is allowed a maximum of seven (7) hours to conclude the PMQ deposition. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Order is signed in Open Court. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Kimberly Jennings, CSR #11452, (650-218-9855) Reported, (501).

Plaintiff Frances Sussna'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Palo Alto Medical Foundation, Gregory Engle, M.D., Dr. Conrad Massimo Vial, And Dr. Graham Beaumont And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-24-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: DAVID SIEGEL, ESQ., 408.255.6310, FOR PLAINTIFF FRANCES SUSSNA.
TIMOTHY DAY, ESQ., 909.307.9380, FOR DEFENDANT PRIME OLD COUNTRY LP.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling with modification: PLAINTIFF FRANCES SUSSNA'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS TO PALO ALTO MEDICAL FOUNDATION, GREGORY ENGLE, M.D., DR. CONRAD MASSIMO VIAL, and DR. GRAHAM BEAUMONT AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS is Granted, in part. The subpoena to Palo Alto Medical Foundation (PAMF) shall be quashed, but may be modified and reissued pursuant to a court order. The subpoena to PAMF, Plaintiff's general purpose healthcare provider, is overbroad in seeking "any and all" records without restriction as to type of medical condition, scope or time. The subpoena to PAMF shall be limited to cardiac (heart) treatment (including stress tests, heart diagnosis, treatment and procedures), obesity, shortness of breath, blood pressure and neurological issues (including myoclonus). The motion to quash subpoenas directed specifically to Dr. Vial and Dr. Engle, Plaintiff's cardiac surgeon and cardiologist, respectively, is denied. Plaintiff has placed her heart condition and heart surgery at issue. The parties are to meet and confer and attempt to agree regarding the time limitation, if any, to be placed on these subpoenas. As to the subpoena to Dr. Graham Beaumont, if Plaintiff can clarify and stipulate that: (1) she does not claim severe emotional distress over and above that which would ordinarily be associated with an injury of this type; and (2) at trial she will claim no economic damages for therapy bills and will not present expert testimony by any mental health provider at trial, then the Court will quash the subpoena to Dr. Graham Beaumont (psychiatrist). If she does not so clarify, a hearing will be required on the subpoena to Dr. Beaumont. Monetary sanctions are denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
